name: Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. v. Dukes
summary: The Supreme Court tightened the requirements for class certification under Rule 23, holding that plaintiffs must demonstrate that there are questions of law or fact truly common to the class — not just common questions but questions whose answers will drive the resolution of the case.
significance: The government relies on this case to argue that the class was improperly certified because the plaintiffs do not share a sufficiently common question of law or fact to justify class-wide treatment.
